messages.content: when was openai founded
OpenAI was founded on December 11, 2015.
who were the founders of openai
The founders of OpenAI are Elon Musk, Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever, John Schulman, and Wojciech Zaremba.
which of these people have left the company?

Elon Musk and Sam Altman have left OpenAI. Elon Musk left the company's board of directors in 2018 due to potential conflicts of interest with his other companies, while Sam Altman stepped down as CEO in 2019 to focus on other projects but remains an advisor to OpenAI.
What was OpenAI's goal?

OpenAI's goal is to create artificial intelligence that is safe and beneficial for humanity. Specifically, the organization is focused on advancing AI research in areas such as natural language processing, robotics, and reinforcement learning, while also promoting open access and safety in the development of AI. The founders of OpenAI believe that AI has the potential to be one of the most transformative technologies of our time, but also recognize the need for careful consideration and ethical frameworks as its capabilities continue to grow.
Didn't they want AI to be free and open source too before things have changed to make their project proprietary?

Yes, OpenAI's original goal was to create artificial intelligence that is free and open to all, which aligns with their mission to ensure that AI technology is safe, beneficial, and accessible to everyone. However, in 2019, OpenAI changed course and announced that they had created an exclusive for-profit company, OpenAI LP, which would focus on developing artificial intelligence for commercial purposes. This decision was made in order to secure additional funding and avoid potential conflicts of interest with their non-profit mission. Nonetheless, OpenAI states that they remain committed to transparency and safety in the development of AI, and regularly publishes research papers and tools that are available for public use.
What was OpenAI's first service to be available to the public?

OpenAI's first service to be available to the public was the OpenAI API, which was launched in June 2020. The OpenAI API provides access to a powerful natural language processing (NLP) model called GPT-3, which is capable of generating human-like language and responding to complex queries. The API allows developers to integrate GPT-3's language capabilities into their own applications and services, enabling new possibilities for chatbots, question-answering systems, and other types of automated language processing. The OpenAI API is available through a subscription-based model, with different pricing tiers depending on the level of access needed.
what is dall-e

DALL·E (pronounced "dolly") is an artificial intelligence model developed by OpenAI, which is capable of generating high-quality digital images from textual descriptions. Like GPT-3, which can process language, DALL·E is able to understand and generate images, making it possible to convert text descriptions into unique visual outputs. The name "DALL·E" refers to the famous surrealist artist Salvador Dali, and Pixar's WALL·E. The model was trained using a large dataset of image-text pairs and can create diverse and creative visualizations of various concepts and objects, ranging from a "toaster with a sesame seed bagel in it" to an "armchair that looks like a pizza."
When did DALL-E start development?

OpenAI started working on DALL·E in 2020, and the model was publicly unveiled in January 2021. The development of DALL·E built upon OpenAI's previous work on GPT-3 and other models, and required significant advances in image processing and generative models to achieve its ability to generate high-quality images from textual descriptions.
But how long has AI text to image generators been in the industry for?

AI text to image generators have been in development for several years, with research in this area dating back over a decade. However, it is only in recent years that significant progress has been made in this field, with the development of more advanced machine learning techniques, larger training datasets, and more powerful computing resources. 

One of the earliest efforts in this area was in 2014 when a team of researchers at the University of Montreal developed a system called GAN-INT-CLS, which used a combination of generative adversarial networks (GANs) and deep learning to generate images from textual descriptions. 

Since then, there have been a number of notable advances in AI text to image generation, including the work being done at OpenAI with DALL·E and other models. However, this remains an active area of research, and there are still many challenges to overcome, such as creating images that are sufficiently varied, realistic, and informative, and ensuring that the generated content does not perpetuate biases or harmful stereotypes.
how small were the images generated with the first image generators developed

The first AI image generators that used a text-to-image approach were capable of generating very small, low-resolution images. For example, the GAN-INT-CLS system developed by researchers at the University of Montreal in 2014 was capable of generating images that were 32x32 pixels in size. This is because generating high-quality images with fine details and realistic textures and lighting requires a large number of pixels and a lot of computational power. 

Since then, however, there have been significant advances in both the size and quality of images that can be generated using AI text-to-image models. DALL·E, for example, is capable of generating high-quality images that are up to 512x512 pixels in size, which is a significant improvement over earlier models.
is there a predecessor to ai

In a sense, yes. The idea of intelligent machines or devices that can perform tasks that normally require human intelligence has been around for thousands of years, with various forms of automation being developed throughout history. A precursor to modern AI was the development of the first computers in the 1940s and 1950s, which were capable of performing logical operations and basic calculations. 

However, the modern field of Artificial Intelligence as we know it today was first established as a scientific discipline in the early 1950s, with the development of the Dartmouth Conference, which brought together researchers from a variety of fields to discuss the potential of machines to perform tasks that require human-like intelligence. The field of AI has since exploded in size and scope, with significant advances in machine learning, natural language processing, robotics, and other subfields that have made AI a ubiquitous technology in today's society.
when did ai explode in popularity

AI has seen several explosions in popularity over the years, but the most recent surge in interest and investment began in the mid to late 2000s and has continued to grow since then. There have been several factors driving this trend, including the convergence of advances in computing power, big data, and machine learning algorithms, the proliferation of digital devices and online platforms, and the increased availability of funding and support for AI research and development from governments, corporations, and venture capitalists.

One of the most significant breakthroughs that helped to spur this growth was the development of deep learning techniques in the mid-2000s, which allowed machines to learn from large datasets and significantly improve their accuracy in tasks such as image and speech recognition. This helped to pave the way for the development of practical AI applications that could be applied to a wide range of fields, from healthcare and finance to transportation and entertainment.

The explosion of AI in recent years has led to significant advances in fields such as robotics, natural language processing, and autonomous vehicle technology, and has sparked new debates and questions around the ethical and social implications of this technology.
what's a late late show

The Late Late Show is a long-running American television talk show that originally premiered in 1995. It is hosted by James Corden and airs on CBS, typically late at night after local news programs. The show features celebrity interviews, comedy sketches, musical performances, and other segments, and has become known for its light-hearted and humorous approach to late-night television. Over the years, The Late Late Show has featured a wide range of guests from various fields, including actors, musicians, athletes, politicians, and social media influencers.
what time is it usually held

The Late Late Show usually airs at 12:37 a.m. Eastern Time/11:37 p.m. Central Time on weeknights (Monday through Friday). However, the specific time may vary depending on the local CBS affiliate, so viewers should consult their local TV listings for the exact broadcast time in their area. The show typically runs for about an hour, although this may vary depending on the episode and the guest lineup.
how about for viewers who are attending in studio

For viewers who are attending The Late Late Show in-studio, the show tapes at CBS Television City in Los Angeles, California, and typically starts at around 4:00 PM Pacific Time. However, the exact time may vary depending on the day and the schedule of that particular episode, so ticket holders are advised to check their ticket information for the exact arrival time. In general, attendees are asked to arrive at the studio at least an hour before the taping begins, to allow enough time for check-in, security screening, and seating. Audience members are typically asked to dress in business casual attire and are prohibited from bringing food, drinks, large bags, or any prohibited items into the studio.
